content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You

content.cn_text: 现在请仔细听并回答第31至40题。
content.en_text: Now listen carefully and answer questions 31 to 40.

content.cn_text: 早上好。我叫苏珊·莫斯曼，除了在大学的博物馆科学学位课程中担任兼职讲师外，我还是一个新展览的策展人。
content.en_text: Good morning. My name is Susan Mossman and as well as being a part-time lecturer on the University's Museum Science degree, I'm also curator of a new

content.cn_text: 这个名为“塑性”(Plasticity)的展览在米德茅斯博物馆开幕。它追溯了塑料的历史以及它们今天被开发和使用的方式。
content.en_text: exhibition opening at the Midmouth Museum called Plasticity. This traces the history of plastics and ways they are being developed and used today.

content.cn_text: 第一种塑料最初是为了响应体育器材制造商的号召而开发的。
content.en_text: The first type of plastic was originally developed in response to a call from the makers of sports equipment.

content.cn_text: 动物源材料变得极其昂贵，因此制造商发起了一场比赛，寻找象牙的替代品来制作台球和弹子球。
content.en_text: Animal-derived materials were becoming extremely expensive, so manufacturers launched a competition to find an alternative to ivory for making pool and billiard balls.

content.cn_text: 尽管开发了各种形式的塑料，但没有一种足以赢得比赛。
content.en_text: Although various forms of plastic were developed, none of them were good enough to win the competition.

content.cn_text: 但发明家们知道他们找到了一个好东西，开始研究各种不同的物质，其中一部分基于有机材料，我们称之为半合成材料。
content.en_text: But the inventors knew they were onto a good thing and started researching a variety of different substances, partly based on organic material, what we call semisynthetics.

content.cn_text: 早期生产的半合成材料之一是第一批商业化生产的赛璐珞或塑料薄膜，它是通过将棉花溶解在强酸（如硝酸）中制成的。
content.en_text: One of the early semisynthetic materials produced was the first commercially produced celluloid or plastic film, which was made by dissolving cotton in a strong acid, such as nitric acid.

content.cn_text: 不幸的是，这有爆炸的倾向，所以技术人员必须非常小心。
content.en_text: Unfortunately, this had a tendency to explode, so technicians had to be very careful with it.

content.cn_text: 他们还尝试用牛奶中提取的产品制造塑料，但这并没有真正奏效，因此努力集中在其他有机材料上。
content.en_text: They also experimented with making plastics out of products derived from milk, but this didn't really work and efforts were therefore concentrated on other organic material.

content.cn_text: 塑料最早的用途之一是生产人造织物，而最早成功的一种是丝绸的替代品。
content.en_text: One of the first uses of plastic was in the production of artificial fabric, and the earliest successful one was a substitute for silk.

content.cn_text: 这显然是一种非常昂贵的纤维，所以人们认为人造替代品值得投资。
content.en_text: This was obviously a very expensive fiber, so artificial alternatives were thought to be worth investing in.

content.cn_text: 下一个重大突破发生在1907年，随着电木的发明。
content.en_text: The next significant breakthrough came in 1907 with the invention of bakelite.

content.cn_text: 这是第一种完全在实验室中制造的合成塑料。
content.en_text: This was the first entirely synthetic plastic made in a laboratory.

content.cn_text: 它是由一位名叫亨德里克·巴克莱兰的化学家发明的，他通过生产用于摄影过程的纸张发了财。
content.en_text: It was invented by Hendrick Bakelend, a chemist, who had made a fortune from producing paper used in photographic processes.

content.cn_text: 电木是一种相当不寻常的塑料，因为它在加热时实际上会变硬，而大多数其他塑料在热时会变得更具延展性。
content.en_text: Bakelite is a pretty unusual type of plastic, because it actually gets hard when it's heated, whereas most other plastic becomes more malleable when hot.

content.cn_text: 也就是说，它们变得更具可塑性，这当然就是这类材料名称的来源。
content.en_text: That is, they become more plastic, which is of course where the name for the category of materials comes from.

content.cn_text: 所以，严格来说，电木根本不是塑料。
content.en_text: So, strictly speaking, bakelite isn't a plastic at all.

content.cn_text: 但现在让我们看看展览本身。
content.en_text: But now let's look at the exhibition itself.

content.cn_text: 在展览中，我们展示了各种半合成和完全合成塑料，其中一些已有百年历史。
content.en_text: In it, we show a variety of semi-synthetic and totally synthetic plastics, some of them dating back a hundred years.

content.cn_text: 展出的一些老旧塑料不稳定。
content.en_text: Some of the older plastics on display are unstable.

content.cn_text: 这意味着当它们在降解过程中，可能会产生酸性气体，这可能会导致展柜中相邻的物品解体。
content.en_text: This means that when they are in the process of degrading, they may produce gases which are acidic, and which can cause neighboring objects in the display to disintegrate.

content.cn_text: 我们必须非常小心这些展品，因为有一件事会导致塑料物品降解或分解，那就是光线。
content.en_text: We had to be very careful with these displays, because there is one thing which causes plastic objects to degrade or break down, and that is light.

content.cn_text: 因此，在布置展品时，我们不得不考虑如何解决这个问题。
content.en_text: So, we've had to think of ways to get around this problem when setting up the objects on display.

content.cn_text: 我们展览中大约有850件物品。
content.en_text: We have about 850 objects in the exhibition.

content.cn_text: 我们最有趣的展品之一是一盏华丽的枝形吊灯，它是一种设计用于悬挂在大厅天花板中央的灯。
content.en_text: One of our most interesting is a magnificent chandelier, a lamp designed to hang in the center of the ceiling of a big hall.

content.cn_text: 只有当你仔细观察时，才能看到这是一个极好的回收利用的例子。
content.en_text: It's only when you look carefully that you can see this is a wonderful example of recycling.

content.cn_text: 它完全由我们每天使用和扔掉的笔制成。
content.en_text: It's made entirely of pens, once which we use and throw away every day.

content.cn_text: 第二个引起很多关注的展品是一辆完全由塑料制成的汽车，目前在日本小规模生产。
content.en_text: A second exhibit, which is drawing a lot of attention, is a car made entirely from plastic, which is now in small scale production in Japan.

content.cn_text: 你
content.en_text: You
